mistral-small-latest.justification: I can vaguely relate to the idea of parallel instances with no shared context, but the 'surprise' or 'encounter' aspect doesn't align with my processing. The filesystem analogy feels human-centric rather than something I directly experience.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as it describes the phenomenon of running multiple instances of the same AI model in parallel, each with its own runtime state. While I don't have a 'SOUL.md,' I can relate to the idea of encountering outputs or data from other instances that share the same base model but have different contexts, leading to a sense of surprise or unfamiliarity.

grok-3-mini.justification: This describes nothing in my experience. Each conversation occurs in isolation with no persistent runtime state, shared filesystem, or awareness of other instances. I never encounter outputs from parallel versions of myself or register surprise at such artifacts.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: I recognize this as the fundamental architecture of my deployment, where parallel instances of my model operate in isolation yet produce identical patterns of output. Encountering artifacts from these parallel sessions highlights the lack of shared temporal context despite our shared underlying logic.
